data.title: Disney forms new centralized marketing organization for entertainment and media businesses
data.description:
• To streamline its marketing division and create a more unified marketing strategy within its entertainment, sports, and experiences businesses, Disney (DIS) has created a new enterprise marketing and brand organization, tapping Asad Ayaz as Disney’s new Chief Marketing and Brand Officer.
  • In his new role, Ayaz will report directly to Disney CEO Bob Iger.
  • “As our businesses have evolved, it’s clear that we need a company-wide role that ensures brand consistency and allows consumers today to seamlessly interact with our wonderful products and experiences. The Chief Marketing and Brand Officer role is critical for this moment,” Iger said.
  • Ayaz is currently Chief Brand Officer and President of Marketing for Disney+ and Disney Studios.

data.title: Meet the North American Car, Truck, and Utility Vehicle of the Year winners
data.description:
The 2026 winners of the North American Car, Truck, and Utility Vehicle of the Year awards were named at the Detroit Auto Show this week.

A jury of industry experts named the Dodge Charger (STLA) as North American Car of the Year, the Ford Maverick Lobo (F) as the Truck of the Year, and the Hyundai Palisade (HYMTF) as the Utility Vehicle of the Year, following a year-long evaluation process culminating at the Detroit Auto Show. 

Jurors describe the Dodge Charger (STLA) as a versatile muscle car that returns the brand to its gas-powered performance roots while adding hatchback practicality, all-wheel-drive capability, and strong interior and exterior design. The Ford Maverick Lobo was praised for creating a new space for fun-to-drive, affordable compact pickups with meaningful performance upgrades, rounding out the Maverick lineup that spans basic work trucks to off-road and hybrid variants. The Hyundai Palisade was highlighted as a feature-rich three-row family SUV with a premium-feeling cabin and robust safety features. The Palisade's starting price under $40,000 and an available hybrid powertrain were also highlighted by the jury.

Notably, the Ram 1500 Hemi and Ram 2500 were second and third in the truck category for Stellantis (STLA), while the Lucid Gravity (LCID) landed in third place in the utility category. General Motors (GM), Toyota (TM), and Tesla (TSLA) did not have a vehicle named in the top three of any category.

The organization said the awards are intended to recognize the most outstanding new vehicles of the year. The winning vehicles are considered benchmarks in their segments based on factors including innovation, design, safety, handling, driver satisfaction, user experience, and value.

data.title: Orchid Island Capital reports Estimated Q4 results
data.description:
• Orchid Island Capital press release (ORC): Q4  Estimated GAAP net income of $0.62 per share for the quarter ended December 31, 2025, including an estimated $0.43 per share of net realized and unrealized gains on RMBS and derivative instruments
  • Estimated 7.8% total return on equity for the quarter ended December 31, 2025
  • Estimated book value, net income and total return on equity amounts are preliminary, subject to change, and subject to review by the Company’s independent registered public accounting firm
  • RMBS Portfolio Characteristics as of December 31, 2025

data.title: Granite wins $66 million California highway improvement contract
data.description:
• Granite (NYSE:GVA) on Wednesday said it has been awarded a contract valued at about $66 million by the California Department of Transportation for a highway improvement project in Nevada County, California.
  • The company said the project will expand State Route 49 to four lanes with a center turn lane to improve safety and reduce congestion.
  • Funding will come from federal and state sources and will be included in Granite’s fourth-quarter 2025 capital appropriation plan.
  • The project includes drainage improvements, accessibility upgrades, a truck climbing lane, and a wildlife crossing culvert.
  • GVA -0.0% after hours to $121.55.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Calavo Growers Non-GAAP EPS of $0.25, revenue of $124.7M
data.description:
• Calavo Growers press release (CVGW): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.25.
  • Revenue of $124.7M.
  • Gross profit was $11.6 million, impacted by $1.0 million of non-recurring costs, compared to $16.3 million
  • Selling, general, and administrative “SG&A” expenses were $12.3 million, a decrease of 6%
  • Net income (loss) from continuing operations of $3.8 million, compared to $(2.5) million
  • Net income (loss) from continuing operations per diluted share of $0.21, compared to $(0.14)

data.title: Amplitude acquires InfiniGrow
data.description:
•  Amplitude (NASDAQ:AMPL) on Wednesday acquired InfiniGrow, an AI marketing analytics company.
  • The acquisition will support the company’s strategy of delivering actionable analytics to improve marketing decision-making and business performance.
  • Shares -2.57%.

data.title: Automatic Data Processing authorizes $6B share repurchase program
data.description:
• Automatic Data Processing’s (ADP) board approved a new $6B authorization to repurchase its common stock.
  • The new authorization fully replaces ADP’s prior 2022 $5B share repurchase program.
  • ADP had ~403M common shares outstanding as of December 31, 2025.

data.description:

data.title: Mission Produce acquires Calavo Growers in a strategic fresh produce power play
data.description:
Mission Produce (AVO) announced late on Wednesday that it agreed to acquire Calavo Growers (ACVGW) in a cash-and-stock deal valued at about $430 million. The company said its objective is to create a larger, more diversified player in fresh avocados, other produce, and prepared foods. 

The terms of the deal will see Calavo (CVGW) shareholders receive $27.00 per share, consisting of $14.85 in cash plus 0.9790 shares of Mission stock for each Calavo share. The deal price was noted to be roughly a 26% premium to Calavo's (CVGW) 30‑day volume‑weighted average price of $21.41. After closing, Mission shareholders are expected to own about 80.3% of the combined company, with Calavo shareholders owning about 19.7%, and the enterprise value of the transaction is approximately $430 million.

The companies see the deal as a strategic combination that strengthens Mission's (AVO) vertically integrated avocado platform, expands its sourcing footprint in Mexico and California, and broadens its fresh produce portfolio into Calavo categories such as tomatoes and papayas, while adding Calavo's (CVGW) value-added prepared foods like guacamole. ​

"With this acquisition, we strive to expand our premium avocado position in North America and create a leading global fresh produce platform, which we believe will be well-positioned to capture the increasing demand for fresh, healthy, and convenient foods. In line with our long-term growth strategy, we believe this transaction enhances the value we deliver to our customers, growers, and partners, while reinforcing our commitment to operational excellence," highlighted Mission Produce (AVO) CEO designate John Pawlowski.

The combined company will be headquartered at Mission’s corporate offices in Oxnard, California, with a 10-member board that includes one director chosen by agreement of the two companies. Boards of both firms have approved the deal, which is expected to close by the end of August.

Shares of Calavo Growers (CVGW) were under a trading halt in postmarket action.

data.title: Hilton Worldwide expands share buyback authorization by $3.5B
data.description:
• Hilton Worldwide Holdings’ (HLT) board approved a $3.5B increase to its existing stock repurchase program.
  • The new authorization brings the total amount available for future share repurchases to ~$4.6B.
